title: XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)
body:
## Definition

**XSS** is injecting malicious scripts into a page so they run in victims’ browsers — stealing cookies, session tokens, or DOM data.

## Why it exists

Attack vector when user-controlled strings become HTML or JS without sanitization.

## How it works

Vectors: `innerHTML`, unsanitized URL params reflected in page, compromised npm package. Defense: escape output, [CSP](concept:content-security-policy), `textContent` over `innerHTML`, framework auto-escaping (JSX).

## In practice

Never trust user HTML; sanitize with library if rich text required; HttpOnly cookies.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[XSS](concept:xss)** is injecting malicious scripts into a page so they run in victims’ browsers — stealing [cookies](concept:cookies), session tokens, or [DOM](concept:dom) data.

## Why it exists

Attack vector when user-controlled strings become HTML or JS without sanitization.

## How it works

Vectors: [innerHTML](concept:inner-html), unsanitized URL params reflected in page, compromised npm package. Defense: escape output, [CSP](concept:content-security-policy), [textContent](concept:text-content) over [innerHTML](concept:inner-html), framework auto-escaping (JSX).

## In practice

Never trust user HTML; sanitize with library if rich text required; HttpOnly [cookies](concept:cookies).
